# Spice Gram-to-Volume Conversion Table

Base recipes in OpenCookbook use **grams as the canonical unit**. For spice
quantities under 10g, a volumetric equivalent (`tsp.` / `tbsp.`) is provided
as a practical fallback for cooks without a scale that reads below 1g.

> **Note:** These conversions are approximations. Density varies by brand,
> grind coarseness, and how packed or aerated the spice is. When precision
> matters (e.g. spice blend ratios), use a scale.

---

## How to Use This Table

When writing a spice ingredient under 10g, look up the spice and append the
nearest fractional teaspoon value in parentheses:

```markdown
* 3g Black Pepper (≈ 3/4 tsp.)
* 6g Fine Sea Salt (≈ 1 tsp.)
```

Round to the nearest practical fraction: `1/8`, `1/4`, `1/3`, `1/2`, `2/3`, `3/4`, `1`.

---

## Conversion Table

All values assume a **level, not heaped** measure.

| Spice | 1 tsp (g) | 1 tbsp (g) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Fine Sea Salt | 6g | 18g | Coarse salt is lighter; kosher salt varies significantly by brand |
| Black Pepper (ground) | 2.3g | 7g | |
| White Pepper (ground) | 2.4g | 7.2g | |
| Cayenne Pepper | 2.3g | 7g | |
| Paprika (sweet or smoked) | 2.3g | 7g | |
| Garlic Powder | 3.1g | 9.3g | |
| Onion Powder | 2.5g | 7.5g | |
| Cumin (ground) | 2.1g | 6.3g | |
| Coriander (ground) | 2.5g | 7.5g | |
| Caraway Seed (ground) | 2.0g | 6g | Whole seeds are lighter |
| Red Pepper Flakes | 2.0g | 6g | Very light; density varies |
| Sumac | 3.0g | 9g | |
| Mustard Seed (ground) | 3.3g | 10g | Whole seeds are heavier (~3.7g/tsp) |
| Turmeric | 3.0g | 9g | |
| Cinnamon (ground) | 2.6g | 7.8g | |
| Allspice (ground) | 1.9g | 5.7g | |
| Cloves (ground) | 2.1g | 6.3g | |
| Ginger (ground) | 1.8g | 5.4g | |
| Nutmeg (ground) | 2.2g | 6.6g | |
| Cardamom (ground) | 2.0g | 6g | |
| Chili Powder (blend) | 2.5g | 7.5g | Check blend composition |
| Za'atar (blend) | 1.5g | 4.5g | Very light due to herb content |
| Brown Sugar (packed) | 4.2g | 12.6g | Packed; not usually a spice but common in rubs |
| Brown Sugar (loose) | 3.0g | 9g | |

---

## Quick Reference: Common Gram Amounts

| Grams | Approximate Volume |
|---|---|
| 1g | ~1/4 tsp. (most ground spices) |
| 2g | ~1/2 tsp. |
| 3g | ~3/4 tsp. |
| 4g | ~1 tsp. (for lighter spices) |
| 5g | ~1 tsp. to 1 1/4 tsp. |
| 6g | ~1 tsp. (salt) or ~1 1/2 tsp. (lighter spices) |
| 7g | ~1 tbsp. (most ground spices) |
| 8g | ~1 tbsp. + 1/4 tsp. |
| 9g | ~1 tbsp. + 1/3 tsp. |
| 10g | ~1 tbsp. + 1/2 tsp. (varies by spice) |

---

## Updating This Table

If a spice used in a recipe is not listed here, look up the density and add a row.
The standard reference is a level teaspoon of the spice, measured on a 0.1g scale,
averaged over at least 3 measures. Include the grind form (ground vs. whole) in the Notes column.
